summary: The main claim is: 5 characters) amounts to 31,728 T tokens This is of course a high estimate and most of this data will clearly have huge compression capacity, but I wanted to get a rough estimate of a high upper bound.
According to these calculations humans "train" on a lot of Petabytes, around 5 Petabytes per year which amounts to ~1000 T tokens.

The main claim is: The first is that one could argue that a Boltzmann brain could not even survive, it would die immediately from lack of oxygen, blood and nutrients.
When I realized that on its own it is impossible (I think) for a Boltzmann brain to survive, an idea came to my mind.
